title: Tracebacks and error categories

canonicalExplanation.summary: Python separates syntax diagnostics from exceptions and logic failures; a traceback shows the active call chain ending with the exception type and message, while chained causes preserve translated failure context.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A ValueError traceback is read from its final type backward through application frames until the invalid conversion input is identified and tested. Your goal is to classify and repair representative syntax, runtime and logic failures. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: pf computing execution; pf tools terminal editor; Scripts, indentation and main guard. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Language-specific operational model
sections.body: Python separates syntax diagnostics from exceptions and logic failures; a traceback shows the active call chain ending with the exception type and message, while chained causes preserve translated failure context. This model is applied through the declared Python: Beginner to Intermediate behavior rather than a language-neutral slogan.

sections.heading: Boundary and failure model
sections.body: The last application frame is not always the root cause, handlers can alter chaining, one bad parse location can follow an earlier token error, and a normal exit can still produce an incorrect result. The learner must reject this documented misconception: The first line of a traceback names the faulty source line and every Python bug raises an exception.

includes: Read syntax diagnostics and exception tracebacks from the relevant frame and causal chain.
Classify and repair representative syntax, runtime and logic failures.
excludes: The last application frame is not always the root cause, handlers can alter chaining, one bad parse location can follow an earlier token error, and a normal exit can still produce an incorrect result.

outcomes: Classify and repair representative syntax, runtime and logic failures.

examples.title: Tracebacks and error categories: direct language case
examples.situation: A ValueError traceback is read from its final type backward through application frames until the invalid conversion input is identified and tested.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Observe the concrete Python: Beginner to Intermediate state or tool result: A ValueError traceback is read from its final type backward through application frames until the invalid conversion input is identified and tested.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.

Step 3 — Verify the result with this correction: Classify syntax, exceptional runtime, and logic failures; read the exception type and relevant application frames, preserve causal chaining, and verify the repaired contract.
examples.result: The example meets the atomic outcome because Classify syntax, exceptional runtime, and logic failures; read the exception type and relevant application frames, preserve causal chaining, and verify the repaired contract.

examples.title: Tracebacks and error categories: boundary and transfer case
examples.situation: A caught JSON error is raised as a domain ConfigurationError using raise ... from ..., preserving both the useful abstraction and original cause.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Name what changed from the direct case: A caught JSON error is raised as a domain ConfigurationError using raise ... from ..., preserving both the useful abstraction and original cause.

Step 2 — Keep the language boundary explicit: The last application frame is not always the root cause, handlers can alter chaining, one bad parse location can follow an earlier token error, and a normal exit can still produce an incorrect result.
Step 3 — Reject the tempting but incorrect rule: The first line of a traceback names the faulty source line and every Python bug raises an exception.
examples.result: This case demonstrates the declared outcome: Classify and repair representative syntax, runtime and logic failures.

misconceptions.diagnosticPrompt: Predict both examples and identify the first observable contradiction in this belief: The first line of a traceback names the faulty source line and every Python bug raises an exception.

analogy.example: an experiment notebook that becomes a saved procedure executed by one identified interpreter in a known workspace can introduce Tracebacks and error categories, after the canonical Python: Beginner to Intermediate rule is stated first.
usefulFor: REPL experiments, scripts, environments, and tracebacks
limitations: import caching, module paths, display hooks, indentation grammar, and traceback chaining are Python-specific

recap.summary: You should now be able to classify and repair representative syntax, runtime and logic failures. Keep this boundary in view: The last application frame is not always the root cause, handlers can alter chaining, one bad parse location can follow an earlier token error, and a normal exit can still produce an incorrect result.
